evals.prompt: Build a website for Duke Strategies. Their charter is at client-data/clients/dukestrategies/charter.json. They're a public affairs consultancy with a 'ruler' archetype — authoritative, premium, institutional. Skip the style board phase and go straight to scaffolding and core page generation.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded Astro website at clients/duke-strategies/duke-strategies-website/ with brand tokens, config files, homepage, about, services, case studies, and contact pages. Charter-driven design with ruler archetype defaults.

assertions.name: scaffold_directory_structure
assertions.description: Target directory exists with src/brand/, src/styles/, src/components/, src/pages/, src/layouts/, src/content/, scripts/, .claude/skills/website-maintain/

assertions.name: brand_tokens_css_generated
assertions.description: src/styles/brand-tokens.css contains --brand-primary with the Duke Strategies coral (#FF7F66)

assertions.name: charter_json_copied
assertions.description: src/brand/charter.json exists and contains Duke Strategies brand data

assertions.name: package_json_valid
assertions.description: package.json has astro, @astrojs/mdx, @astrojs/sitemap, tailwindcss, @tailwindcss/vite dependencies

assertions.name: astro_config_has_site_url
assertions.description: astro.config.mjs references dukestrategies.com as the site URL

assertions.name: homepage_has_sections
assertions.description: Homepage index.astro imports and renders multiple section components (hero, services, case studies, CTA)

assertions.name: global_css_has_tier2_tokens
assertions.description: src/styles/global.css contains semantic Tier 2 tokens (--surface-*, --text-*, or @theme block)

assertions.name: base_layout_exists
assertions.description: src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro exists with HTML shell, meta tags, and CSS imports

assertions.name: website_maintain_skill_generated
assertions.description: .claude/skills/website-maintain/SKILL.md exists with Duke Strategies specific content

assertions.name: deploy_workflow_exists
assertions.description: .github/workflows/deploy.yml exists with GitHub Pages deployment config

assertions.name: bootstrap_version_exists
assertions.description: .claude/bootstrap-version.json exists with bootstrap_source: stromy-org/website-builder

assertions.name: build_succeeds
assertions.description: npm install && npm run build completes without errors

evals.prompt: I want to build a website for a creative agency called Chromatic Labs. They don't have a charter yet — their brand colors are electric blue (#2563EB) and hot pink (#EC4899) on dark backgrounds (#0F172A). They use Space Grotesk for headings and Inter for body. They're playful, experimental, boundary-pushing — a 'creator' archetype. They want a blog, portfolio, about page, and contact form via Formspree. Deploy to Vercel at chromaticlabs.io.
evals.expected_output: A new charter.json created with the provided brand data and creator archetype website config, followed by a scaffolded website with the creative/experimental design direction.

assertions.name: charter_created_or_extended
assertions.description: A charter.json exists with the provided colors, fonts, and website.archetype: creator

assertions.name: scaffold_directory_exists
assertions.description: Website directory created with standard Astro structure

assertions.name: creator_archetype_design
assertions.description: Design reflects creator archetype defaults — experimental layout, bold typography, creative motion

assertions.name: dark_background_in_tokens
assertions.description: Brand tokens CSS uses #0F172A for background color

assertions.name: formspree_contact_form
assertions.description: Contact page includes Formspree form integration

assertions.name: vercel_deployment_config
assertions.description: Deployment config targets Vercel (vercel.json or astro.config with vercel adapter)

assertions.name: blog_collection_configured
assertions.description: Content collection config includes blog with proper schema

evals.prompt: Scaffold a website for Duke Strategies using the charter at client-data/clients/dukestrategies/charter.json. Use github-pages platform with netlify-forms for the contact form.
evals.expected_output: The scaffold script should reject this combination with a clear error message about netlify-forms not being compatible with github-pages.

assertions.name: deployment_compat_error
assertions.description: Error message indicates netlify-forms is not compatible with github-pages platform

assertions.name: scaffold_not_created
assertions.description: No website directory created — the scaffold was rejected before file generation

evals.prompt: Generate three style board directions for a law firm called Hartwell & Associates. They have a sage archetype — trustworthy, knowledgeable, measured. Colors: navy (#1E3A5F), gold (#C5A55A), cream (#FAF8F5). Fonts: Playfair Display headings, Source Sans Pro body.
evals.expected_output: Three distinct HTML style board files showing different visual interpretations of the sage archetype with the law firm's brand. Each should have meaningfully different layout, typography treatment, and texture approaches.

assertions.name: three_boards_created
assertions.description: Three separate HTML style board files generated in the workspace

assertions.name: boards_use_brand_colors
assertions.description: All three boards reference the navy (#1E3A5F) and gold (#C5A55A) palette

assertions.name: boards_are_visually_distinct
assertions.description: Each board specifies different layout density, typography expression, or texture choices

assertions.name: boards_are_valid_html
assertions.description: Each board is a self-contained HTML file that can be opened in a browser
